locator: IEEE Xplore conference document abstract 11115541
notes: Conference proceeding; tiered T2 (unverified journal peer review). The RMB/GWh savings claim is vendor/lab-asserted and not independently triangulated this session.

title: 高功率高效率速调管输出耦合器的优化与设计 (Optimization and Design of a High-Power High-Efficiency Klystron Output Coupler)
authors_or_org: Lu Zhijun; Zhou Zusheng; Xiao Ouzheng; Li Xiao
claim_supported: Reports single-beam klystron output-coupler CW power testing exceeding 690 kW and a multi-beam rectangular-waveguide-window coupler reaching 115 kW, using T-bar transition structures to optimize field distribution near ceramic windows; targets 650 MHz/800 kW CW klystrons for fusion and high-energy-physics use. Published in High Power Laser and Particle Beams (强激光与粒子束), 2024.
locator: Full article HTML, hplpb.com.cn DOI page (fetched directly)
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session; Chinese-language peer-reviewed journal (China Academy of Engineering Physics-affiliated journal). Coupler-failure vulnerability is described as one of the most common klystron/VED failure modes -- relevant to a later bottleneck/pain-point analysis.

title: 47 CFR Part 18 -- Industrial, Scientific, and Medical Equipment
authors_or_org: U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
claim_supported: FCC Part 18 defines the technical standards and designated ISM (industrial, scientific, medical) frequency bands -- including 915 MHz +/-13 MHz and 2450 MHz +/-50 MHz, the bands used by essentially all industrial magnetron heating/curing/drying equipment in this lane -- and compliance testing methods (FCC MP-5).
locator: eCFR Title 47, Chapter I, Subchapter A, Part 18 (current version)
notes: Official current regulation text (eCFR, government-hosted); page found via search, not independently WebFetched in full this session.

title: ISO 11137-1:2025 -- Sterilization of health care products -- Radiation -- Part 1: Requirements for the development, validation and routine control of a sterilization process for medical devices
authors_or_org: International Organization for Standardization (ISO)
claim_supported: Second edition (2025) of ISO 11137-1, replacing the 2006 first edition, sets requirements for developing, validating, and routinely controlling radiation sterilization processes (Co-60/Cs-137 gamma, electron-beam, X-ray) for medical devices; adds normative references to ISO 13004 and ASTM 52628 for cross-standard terminology consistency.
locator: ISO standard catalogue page 81721 (iso.org)
notes: Standard itself is paywalled; bibliographic/scope detail taken from ISO catalogue page and secondary summaries (International Irradiation Association news item) found via WebSearch. Not independently WebFetched in full this session.

title: IEC 60060-1:2025 -- High-voltage test techniques -- Part 1: General terminology and test requirements
authors_or_org: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
claim_supported: Fourth edition (2025) of IEC 60060-1 defines general terminology and test requirements for dielectric testing with direct, alternating, impulse, and combined high voltages (equipment Um above 1.0 kV AC / 1.5 kV DC), including the standard 1.2/50 microsecond lightning-impulse and 250/2500 microsecond switching-impulse waveforms used to qualify high-voltage pulsed-power components.
locator: ANSI webstore standard listing page (IEC 60060-1 Ed. 4.0, 2025)
notes: Standard itself is paywalled; bibliographic/scope detail from ANSI/GlobalSpec catalogue listings found via WebSearch. Not independently WebFetched in full this session.

title: Pulsed-Power Science and Technology -- Key Research Facilities (Z Pulsed Power Facility)
authors_or_org: Sandia National Laboratories
claim_supported: Sandia's Z Pulsed Power Facility ('Z machine') is described as Earth's most powerful pulsed-power/X-ray-generating facility, driving >20 million-amp, ~100-nanosecond current pulses through wire arrays; Sandia's roadmap describes a future 'ZN' (Z Neutron) upgrade targeting 20-30 MJ of fusion yield per shot using linear-transformer-driver (LTD) technology to replace the current Marx-generator banks.
locator: Sandia National Laboratories Pulsed-Power Science and Technology program page
notes: National-lab primary program page; found via search, not independently WebFetched in full this session.

title: 光源組>線型加速器小組 (Light Source Division > Linear Accelerator Team) -- pulse power systems overview
authors_or_org: National Synchrotron Radiation Research Center (國家同步輻射研究中心, NSRRC), Taiwan
claim_supported: NSRRC's Linear Accelerator Team describes self-developed (in-house) pulse-power systems -- including kicker-magnet and septum-magnet pulse power -- with 12 such pulse-power systems deployed at the Taiwan Light Source (TLS) and 12 at the Taiwan Photon Source (TPS).
locator: NSRRC organizational page (Chinese-language), Dept_UID=37
notes: Direct WebFetch attempt failed this session (connection reset); bibliographic/content detail taken from WebSearch synthesis of the same page. Taiwan is a national synchrotron-facility in-house pulsed-power developer rather than a commercial-vendor buyer in this instance.

title: 大電力高周波源 (High-Power RF Sources) -- OHO summer-school lecture text
authors_or_org: High Energy Accelerator Research Organization (KEK), Japan
claim_supported: KEK-authored Japanese-language technical lecture text on high-power RF sources for accelerators, covering klystron fundamentals and their role in RF systems for KEK linac/collider injectors (context for KEKB, TRISTAN, and related Japanese accelerator RF infrastructure).
locator: KEK OHO summer-school lecture PDF, accwww2.kek.jp
notes: Educational/technical lecture material from a national laboratory, not a peer-reviewed paper -- tiered T2 (national-lab primary technical document, not journal-refereed). Not independently WebFetched this session (content summarized via WebSearch of KEK-hosted pages).

title: K-Series Klystron Modulators (K100/K200/K300/K400/K500) -- product datasheets
authors_or_org: ScandiNova Systems AB
claim_supported: ScandiNova's K-Series solid-state klystron modulators span 3-100 MW RF peak power across five product tiers (K100 through K500/EK500), marketed on lower cooling cost (about half of line-type PFN modulators) and reduced unscheduled downtime versus legacy vacuum-tube/PFN modulator technology.
locator: K-Series product family page and individual model datasheets, scandinovasystems.com
notes: Vendor claims (efficiency/cost-of-ownership comparisons) are vendor-asserted, not independently audited. No public pricing found this session.

title: Industrial magnetron product line (900 W - 100 kW, 915 MHz and 2450 MHz) -- datasheets and complete generator systems
authors_or_org: Muegge GmbH (Muegge Group)
claim_supported: Muegge (Germany) sells industrial magnetrons spanning 915 MHz (5-100 kW output) and 2450 MHz (800 W-15 kW output) bands, plus complete industrial microwave generator systems (e.g., a 30 kW/915 MHz system with full WR975 waveguide chain) for heating, drying, food processing, and chemical processing applications; a used 30 kW/915 MHz Muegge system was separately listed for resale on the Exapro industrial-equipment marketplace, giving an indirect secondary-market price reference point.
locator: Product category page and individual magnetron/system datasheets, muegge-group.com; secondary resale listing on exapro.com
notes: No new-unit list price found this session (vendor requires direct quote); secondary-market listing (Exapro) is the closest price signal found.

title: NPCT (New Parametric Current Transformer) and CR-BCM beam-current-monitor datasheets and price list
authors_or_org: Bergoz Instrumentation
claim_supported: Bergoz Instrumentation (France, founded 1981) manufactures non-destructive beam-current-measurement instrumentation used on most of the world's particle accelerators; its NPCT (New Parametric Current Transformer, full-scale ranges +/-20 mA to +/-20 A) and CR-BCM (cavity-resonator beam current monitor, down to 1 nA with 100 pA resolution at 500 Hz) product lines publish datasheets and price lists directly on the company site (with a stated caveat that US-bound shipments may add tariffs).
locator: NPCT and CR-BCM product/datasheet pages, bergoz.com
notes: Explicit vendor statement that a price list exists is a stronger-than-typical market-industry signal (named price list), though exact figures were not extracted this session.

title: Communications & Power Industries (CPI) -- corporate and product overview
authors_or_org: Communications & Power Industries LLC (CPI)
claim_supported: CPI (Palo Alto, CA; descended from Varian Associates' original 1948 klystron business, spun out in 1995) is a ~$733M-revenue, ~1,618-employee incumbent manufacturer spanning klystrons, TWTs/TWTAs, gyrotrons, magnetrons, SSPAs, IOTs, and medical X-ray generators for communications and defense markets; owned by Veritas Capital since a ~$525M acquisition in February 2011 (previously a public company, IPO April 2006).
locator: Company website (cpii.com) and corroborating RocketReach/ZoomInfo/Wikipedia company-profile pages; historical SEC 10-K/8-K filings (2003-2004) also located under prior public-company status
notes: Revenue/employee figures from third-party aggregator (RocketReach) rather than an audited CPI filing directly (CPI is currently privately held under Veritas Capital, so no current public 10-K exists); tiered T2 (credible but not company-filed) accordingly. Older CPI International 10-K/8-K SEC filings (2003-2004, pre-privatization) were located and could serve as a T1 historical-filing supplement for a future wave.

title: Design and characterization of 50 kW solid-state RF amplifier
authors_or_org: Authors not resolved this session (International Journal of Microwave and Wireless Technologies, Cambridge Core record)
claim_supported: Reports the design and characterization of a 50 kW solid-state RF power amplifier (accelerator-relevant power-combining architecture), published in International Journal of Microwave and Wireless Technologies (Cambridge Core).
locator: Abstract page, Int. J. Microwave and Wireless Technologies (Cambridge Core)
notes: DOI not resolved this session (abstract-only access); companion paper to L05-007 and L05-052 in the same journal's ESS/accelerator solid-state RF series.

title: The conceptual design of the high-efficiency 400 kW solid-state power station at 352 MHz for the European spallation source
authors_or_org: Authors not resolved this session (International Journal of Microwave and Wireless Technologies, Cambridge Core record; European Spallation Source RF group)
claim_supported: Presents the conceptual design of a 400 kW, 352 MHz high-efficiency solid-state RF power station for the European Spallation Source (combining 12x8 kW amplifier modules to 100 kW then a further waveguide-combining stage toward 400 kW), published in International Journal of Microwave and Wireless Technologies (Cambridge Core).
locator: Abstract page, Int. J. Microwave and Wireless Technologies (Cambridge Core)
notes: DOI not resolved this session (abstract-only access); directly related to the ESS envelope-tracking amplifier work in L05-007. Geography (Sweden) reflects ESS host-site location; author affiliations not independently confirmed this session.

title: High power experiments of the Prototype Solid State RF System for IFMIF-DONES
authors_or_org: Authors not resolved this session (Fusion Engineering and Design, ScienceDirect record; IFMIF-DONES RF group)
claim_supported: Reports high-power test results from a prototype fully solid-state RF system built for IFMIF-DONES (Granada, Spain), an earlier-stage companion result to the 2025 series-production status described in L05-008. Fusion Engineering and Design (2023).
locator: Abstract, Fusion Engineering and Design (ScienceDirect), pii S0920379623000595
notes: DOI not resolved this session (ScienceDirect abstract-only access).
